name: Mirandese

ancestors.notes: Old Portuguese (Aviz-period) adstrate: as Portuguese consolidated as the state language, the Leonese-speaking communities of Miranda, Rionor, and Guadramil faced intensifying pressure. Portuguese orthographic conventions (lh, nh, รฃ) penetrated the writing tradition.

notes: Mirandese (mirandรฉs), spoken in the Miranda do Douro municipality, northeastern Portugal. Classified as Asturleonese. Recognised as a regional/minority language in Portugal since 1999. Retains archaic Asturleonese features: diphthongisation of Latin short /e/ and /o/ (written โจiรช/ieโฉ, โจuรด/uoโฉ), palatal lateral /ส/ preserved (written โจlhโฉ in all positions; word-initial โจlโฉ before vowel = /ส/ in traditional lexicon per Convenรงรฃo ยง L). Heavy Portuguese adstrate via official education and administration. Mirandese preserves the medieval four-way+ sibilant contrast that Portuguese lost, the hallmark of the language: (1) voiceless apico-alveolar /sฬบ/ โจs, ssโฉ and its voiced intervocalic counterpart /zฬบ/ โจ-s-โฉ; (2) voiceless (pre)dorso-dental /s/ โจc (before e/i), รงโฉ and voiced /z/ โจzโฉ; (3) postalveolar /ส/ โจxโฉ and /ส/ โจj, g (before e/i)โฉ; (4) the affricate /tส/ โจchโฉ (still an affricate, unlike modern European Portuguese). NOTATION CONVENTION (matches the TigreGotico/mirandese_g2p expert gold): the apical/dorsal CONTRAST is written by marking only the apical series with the ฬบ diacritic (โจsโฉ = /sฬบ/, /zฬบ/) and leaving the dorso-dental series unmarked (โจc, รง, zโฉ = plain /s/, /z/); this is IPA-standard, keeps the phonemic contrast explicit, and avoids the over-narrow laminal ฬป diacritic the gold does not use. โจรงโฉ is a fricative, not an affricate, and occurs word-finally (rapaรง, lhuรง) where Portuguese writes -z. โจjโฉ is /ส/ (western Iberian, never the Asturian palatal ส) โ โจyโฉ is the glide /j/. INITIAL-L PALATALISATION (Leonese): word-initial โจlโฉ before a vowel = /ส/ (luรง โ [หสus], Convenรงรฃo ยง L; bare โจlโฉ /l/ survives only in loanwords, proper nouns and the article โ a documented residual limit); โจlhโฉ = /ส/ and โจnhโฉ = /ษฒ/. The trigraphs โจanh, enh, inh, onh, unhโฉ are given explicitly (oral vowel + /ษฒ/, e.g. danho โ [หdaษฒu]) so the greedy nasal-vowel digraph does not swallow the /ษฒ/ of a following โจnhโฉ. Nasal digraphs โจan, en, in, on, unโฉ realise Portuguese-adstrate nasal vowels /ษฬ แบฝ ฤฉ รต ลฉ/ before a consonant or at word boundary (brando โ [หbษพษฬdu]); before a vowel they are oral vowel + /n/. ALLOPHONY (post-lexical, allophone_rules): coda /n/ assimilates in place to a following labial ([m]) or velar ([ล]). Ibero-Romance intervocalic voiced-stop spirantisation (โจb d gโฉ โ [ฮฒ รฐ ษฃ]) is a REAL native process โ voiced stops lenite to [ฮฒ รฐ ษฃ] everywhere except after a pause or a nasal (Mateus & d'Andrade 2000:11; Ferreira & Raposo 1999; mwl Wikipedia phonology, "voiced stops /b d ษก/ MAY be lenited as fricatives [ฮฒ รฐ ษฃ]"). It is applied PER PHONEME on the basis of the expert-human TigreGotico/mirandese_g2p gold measured at true oral-intervocalic positions (post-nasal excluded): /b/ is spirant-dominant (ฮฒ:9/b:5), so MWL_SPIRANT_B rewrites intervocalic /b/โ[ฮฒ] (a net win on the human gold, PER 0.1901โ0.1851). /ษก/ is already spirantised to [ษฃ] intervocalically at the positional_graphemes layer (โจgโฉ intervocalic โ [ษฃ]), so no allophone rule is needed โ adding one only misfires after a diphthong glide (eigual โ gold stop [ษjหษกwal]). /d/, however, remains STOP-dominant intervocalically in the gold (d:13/รฐ:7) โ the documented Asturleonese conservatism/occlusion of /d/, and the reason the Wikipedia wording is hedged ("may be") โ so /d/ keeps its positional default [d] and /d/โ[รฐ] is DELIBERATELY NOT applied as a rewrite (a blanket /d/ rule regresses this expert gold); [รฐ] stays declared in the `allophones` inventory for the lattice / downstream mwl_phonemizer where lexical conditioning is available. Word-onset and post-nasal /b d ษก/ keep the stop.

sources.author: Mateus, M.H.M. & d'Andrade, E.
sources.title: The Phonology of Portuguese
sources.notes: p. 11: the voiced stops /b d ษก/ are lenited to the fricatives/approximants [ฮฒ รฐ ษฃ] in all positions EXCEPT after a pause or a nasal, where they surface as stops [b d ษก]. Grounds the intervocalic spirantisation rule (MWL_SPIRANT_B) and the post-nasal/onset stop exception; the same process is described for Mirandese by Ferreira & Raposo 1999 and the mwl Wikipedia phonology ("voiced stops /b d ษก/ may be lenited as fricatives [ฮฒ รฐ ษฃ]").

sources.notes: Official orthography. ยง L: word-initial l before vowel = /ส/ in traditional lexicon (bare l occurs only in loanwords/proper nouns/article). ยง Nasalidade: an/en/in/on/un are nasal-vowel digraphs at word boundaries; they do NOT nasalise sequences before onset consonants. ยง Sibilantes: six-way contrast sฬบ/zฬบ/sฬป/zฬป/ส/ส. ยง Ditongos: iรช/ie = /je/ (stressed /jษ/); uรด/uo = /wo/ (stressed /wษ/). ยง Acento: paroxytone default; oxytone for -r/-l/-z/-รง/-in/-un/-on/-is/-us/-ns/-รฃo endings.

sources.notes: February 2000 addendum. Key provision: Sendinese speakers may optionally write word-initial l- (not lh-) to represent /l/ rather than /ส/ (e.g., luna/lhuna). Sendinese monophthongisation of iรชโi and uรดโu also codified. Source: secondary (studosmirandeses.blogs.sapo.pt, cursodemirandes.wordpress.com); standalone PDF not located.

stress.notes: Convenรงรฃo Ortogrรกfica da Lรญngua Mirandesa (1999) accentuation, Portuguese-based: paroxytone default; written accent (acute or tilde) overrides. Oxytone-attracting endings reflect Mirandese orthography: consonant-final r/l/z/รง (rapaรง, Pertual, falar) and the final nasals written with -n in the Asturleonese manner โ -in (camin, Sendin), -un, and the systematically oxytone -on class from Latin -ลnem (naรงon, coraรงon, Associaรงon). Unmarked -an/-en verb endings stay paroxytone as in Portuguese -am/-em.

allophone_rules.notes: Nasal place assimilation (Mateus & d'Andrade 2000 ยง3; general Ibero-Romance): a coda /n/ agrees in place with a following labial stop/nasal, surfacing as [m].
Nasal place assimilation (Mateus & d'Andrade 2000 ยง3): a coda /n/ agrees in place with a following velar stop, surfacing as [ล].

Intervocalic spirantisation, /b/ โ [ฮฒ] between vowels (rabudo โ [rษหฮฒudu], haber โ [ษหฮฒeษพ], nuobo โ [หnwoฮฒu]). This is the general Ibero-Romance lenition of voiced stops: in northern/central EP and Mirandese, /b d ษก/ are realised as the fricatives/approximants [ฮฒ รฐ ษฃ] everywhere EXCEPT after a pause or a nasal (Mateus & d'Andrade 2000:11), which is why this rule is conditioned on a preceding vowel (the MWL_NASAL_* environments and word onset keep the stop). The Mirandese-specific process is attested by Ferreira & Raposo 1999 and the mwl Wikipedia phonology ("voiced stops /b d ษก/ may be lenited as fricatives [ฮฒ รฐ ษฃ]"). Applied per-phoneme on the evidence of the expert-human TigreGotico/mirandese_g2p gold, measured at true oral-intervocalic positions (post-nasal excluded): /b/ is spirant-dominant (ฮฒ:9 vs b:5), so this rule is a net win on the human gold (11 words corrected vs 6 lexically-conservative loan stops such as 'brabo', 'alternatibo' left over-spirantised โ the residual lexical variability the downstream mwl_phonemizer can condition on). /ษก/ is NOT given an allophone rule: intervocalic โจgโฉ is already spirantised to [ษฃ] at the positional_graphemes layer, and an additional /ษก/โ[ษฃ] rewrite misfires after the glide of a falling diphthong (eigual โ gold [ษjหษกwal], stop retained). /d/ is NOT spirantised: intervocalic /d/ is stop-dominant in the gold (d:13 vs รฐ:7), the documented Asturleonese conservatism of /d/, so its positional default correctly stays [d] and [รฐ] remains only a lattice candidate.
